title: erysimum × hybridum
intro: Erysimum × hybridum, also known as Wallflower, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, known for its bright yellow, orange, or purple flowers. Traditionally, Wallflower has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe respiratory issues and promote the healing of wounds. The plant's flowers and seeds are rich in vitamin C and other nutrients, providing various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Wallflower is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and coughs to skin conditions and menstrual cramps.
